heading: Agreements with institutions; selection of recipients
label: 1070b–2 Agreements with institutions; selection of recipients

Amendments2020—Subsec. (c)(2)(B). substituted “student aid indexes” for “expected family contributions”.Pub. L. 116–2601998—Subsec. (d). amended heading and text of subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “If the institution’s allocation under this subpart is directly or indirectly based in part on the financial need demonstrated by students who are independent students or attending the institution less than full time and if the total financial need of all such students attending the institution exceeds 5 percent of the total financial need of all students attending such institution, then at least 5 percent of such allotment shall be made available to such students.”Pub. L. 105–2441993—Subsec. (d). substituted “and” for “, a reasonable proportion of the institution’s allocation shall be made available to such students, except that” and “5 percent of the total financial need” for “5 percent of the need”.Pub. L. 103–2081992—Subsec. (a)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “agrees that the Federal share of awards under this subpart will not exceed—Pub. L. 102–325, § 403(d)“(A) 95 percent of such awards in fiscal year 1989,“(B) 90 percent of such awards in fiscal year 1990, and“(C) 85 percent of such awards in fiscal year 1991,except that the Federal share may be exceeded if the Secretary determines, pursuant to regulations establishing objective criteria for such determinations, that a larger Federal share is required to further the purpose of this subpart; and”.Subsec. (d). , inserted “who are independent students or” after “demonstrated by students” and inserted before period at end “, except that if the total financial need of all such students attending the institution exceeds 5 percent of the need of all students attending such institution, then at least 5 percent of such allotment shall be made available to such students”.Pub. L. 102–325, § 403(e)Subsec. (e). , struck out before period at end “, and may transfer such funds in accordance with the provisions of ”.Pub. L. 102–325, § 403(f)section 1095 of this title

agrees that the Federal share of awards under this subpart will not exceed 75 percent of such awards, except that the Federal share may be exceeded if the Secretary determines, pursuant to regulations establishing objective criteria for such determinations, that a larger Federal share is required to further the purpose of this subpart; and

children.heading: Selection of individuals and determination of amount of awards
children.label: c Selection of individuals and determination of amount of awards

children.text: From among individuals who are eligible for supplemental grants for each fiscal year, the institution shall, in accordance with the agreement under , and within the amount allocated to the institution for that purpose for that year under , select individuals who are to be awarded such grants and determine, in accordance with , the amounts to be paid to them.section 1094 of this titlesection 1070b–3 of this titlesection 1070b–1 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: will be designed to award supplemental grants under this subpart, first, to students with exceptional need, and

children.label: ii
children.text: will give a priority for supplemental grants under this subpart to students who receive Pell Grants and meet the requirements of .section 1091 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: For the purpose of subparagraph (A), the term “students with exceptional need” means students with the lowest student aid indexes at the institution.

children.heading: Use of funds for less-than-full-time students
children.label: d Use of funds for less-than-full-time students
children.text: If the institution’s allocation under this subpart is directly or indirectly based in part on the financial need demonstrated by students who are independent students or attending the institution on less than a full-time basis, then a reasonable proportion of the allocation shall be made available to such students.

children.heading: Use and transfer of funds for administrative expenses
children.label: e Use and transfer of funds for administrative expenses
children.text: An agreement entered into pursuant to this section shall provide that funds granted to an institution of higher education may be used only to make payments to students participating in a grant program authorized under this subpart, except that an institution may use a portion of the sums allocated to it under this subpart to meet administrative expenses in accordance with .section 1096 of this title
